{"id":"agent-platform-7tq","title":"Add DB transaction support with atomic rollback","description":"Add explicit DB transaction support to ensure atomicity for multi-step database operations.

Current state: All DB operations are individual statements with no transaction wrapping. A failure mid-operation (e.g. create agent then link skills) leaves partial data.

Implementation:
- Add a `withTransaction(db, fn)` utility in packages/db that wraps operations in BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK
- Use better-sqlite3's `db.transaction()` API (synchronous, automatic rollback on throw)
- Wrap all multi-step mutations: agent creation with relationships, session creation, bulk updates
- Ensure constraint violations (FK, UNIQUE) trigger proper rollback
- Return meaningful error codes to the HTTP layer on rollback
- Add tests verifying rollback on partial failure

This is foundational for agent-platform-5pa (UUID migration) where creating a resource + slug + relationships must be atomic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jasonwilliams2308@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T05:08:11Z","created_by":"Jason Williams","updated_at":"2026-04-16T05:08:11Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"agent-platform-426","title":"Agent execution error recovery and state management","description":"Add error recovery and state management for agent execution (LLM graph runtime).

Problem: If the harness fails mid-execution (LLM timeout, tool call error, MCP server unreachable, DB read failure), the session is left in an inconsistent state with no record of what succeeded or failed. No retry logic exists.

Requirements:
- Track execution state per graph run (which node completed, which failed, error details)
- Persist execution checkpoints so a failed run can be inspected or resumed
- Implement retry logic for transient failures (network timeouts, rate limits) with exponential backoff
- Define which failures are retryable vs terminal (e.g. LLM timeout = retry, schema mismatch = terminal)
- Ensure partial tool call results don't corrupt session state
- Add a session status field (idle, running, failed, completed) so the frontend knows the current state
- Log structured error context (correlation ID, step number, tool name, error type) for debugging

This is about runtime resilience — the DB transaction task (agent-platform-7tq) covers write atomicity, this covers the orchestration layer.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jasonwilliams2308@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T05:09:08Z","created_by":"Jason Williams","updated_at":"2026-04-16T05:09:08Z"}

{"id":"agent-platform-6db","title":"UUID-based referential integrity for all resource relationships","description":"Ensure all cross-resource relationships reference UUIDs, not user-provided names or slugs.

Currently agents store allowed skills/tools/MCP servers as arrays of string IDs. After agent-platform-5pa introduces system-generated UUIDs, these references must use UUIDs consistently:

- `agents.allowedSkillIds` → array of skill UUIDs
- `agents.allowedToolIds` → array of tool UUIDs  
- `agents.allowedMcpServerIds` → array of MCP server UUIDs
- `sessions.agentId` → agent UUID
- Any future join tables or FK columns

Frontend pattern: UI displays human-readable names/slugs but passes UUIDs to the backend for all mutations and lookups. Renaming a resource never breaks relationships.

{"id":"agent-platform-4jh","title":"Streaming backpressure: cancel execution on client disconnect","description":"When a client disconnects mid-stream (closes browser, network drop), the harness continues running the LLM graph and writing to a dead socket. This wastes tokens and compute.

Implement:
- Detect client disconnect via Express `req.on('close')` event
- Propagate an AbortSignal through the harness graph execution
- Cancel in-flight LLM calls (Vercel AI SDK supports AbortSignal)
- Cancel pending tool calls
- Clean up session state (mark as interrupted, not completed)
- Log the cancellation with correlation ID for debugging

This is important for cost control — a disconnected client shouldn't burn through API credits.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jasonwilliams2308@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T05:15:44Z","created_by":"Jason Williams","updated_at":"2026-04-16T05:15:44Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-d8u.md\n\nAdd in-process session lock to prevent concurrent chat requests on same session from corrupting message history. Queue or return 409 Conflict. Interface supports future distributed lock."}
old_value:
{"id":"agent-platform-d8u","title":"Concurrent session safety: prevent interleaved requests","description":"Nothing prevents two simultaneous requests to the same session. Two chat messages arriving at the same time could interleave LLM calls and corrupt conversation history.

Implement:
- Per-session mutex/lock — second request to an active session gets queued or rejected
- Option A (queue): hold the second request until the first completes, then process it
- Option B (reject): return 409 Conflict with a clear message ("session is busy")
- Track session execution state (idle/running) in DB or in-memory map
- Release lock on completion, error, or timeout
- Consider this for the session status field planned in agent-platform-426

Start with Option B (reject) for simplicity — it's safer and the frontend can show "agent is thinking".","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jasonwilliams2308@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T05:15:44Z","created_by":"Jason Williams","updated_at":"2026-04-16T05:15:44Z"}

Built-in (zero config):
- Request latency histogram (per route, per method)
- Error rate counters (by error code)
- Token usage tracking (per session, per model)
- Active session gauge
- Expose metrics at GET /metrics in Prometheus text format
- Enrich /health with DB connectivity check, uptime, active session count

Plugin SDK extension:
- Add `onMetric(name: string, value: number, labels: Record<string, string>)` hook
- Built-in metrics emit through this hook so plugins can intercept
- Add `onHealthCheck()` hook so plugins can contribute health status
- Document the hook interface for third-party plugin authors

This builds on packages/logger (structured logs), packages/plugin-sdk (hook system), and packages/plugin-observability (currently just logging — extend to metrics).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jasonwilliams2308@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T05:12:00Z","created_by":"Jason Williams","updated_at":"2026-04-16T05:12:00Z"}

- Support liveness vs readiness distinction for Kubernetes/Docker

  - /health/ready — dependencies are available (checks DB, etc)
- Expose via plugin onHealthCheck hook (from agent-platform-hkn)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jasonwilliams2308@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T05:15:44Z","created_by":"Jason Williams","updated_at":"2026-04-16T05:15:44Z"}

{"id":"agent-platform-3kd","title":"Design spike: plugin sandboxing and permission model","description":"Plugins currently run in-process with full access to the Node.js runtime. A badly written or malicious plugin could crash the server, access env vars, or read the filesystem.

This needs a design discussion before implementation:
- Should plugins run in worker threads? Separate processes? V8 isolates?
- What capabilities should plugins have access to? (DB? Network? Filesystem?)
- Performance implications of sandboxing (serialization overhead, latency)
- Permission model: declare capabilities in plugin manifest, enforce at runtime?
- How does this interact with the plugin override pattern (memory, observability)?

Create as a design spike — research options, write a short ADR, then break into implementation tasks.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jasonwilliams2308@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-16T05:16:03Z","created_by":"Jason Williams","updated_at":"2026-04-16T05:16:03Z"}
